content.method: Geospatial demographic projection modeling combining municipal-level birth-cohort data with statutory class-size thresholds (26-34 band); scenario analysis under low/medium/high fertility and migration assumptions; overlay with existing selective-track location data to identify settlements at risk.
content.question: At what local cohort size do peripheral Hungarian settlements become unable to sustain even one viable selective-track intake class, and how many settlements are projected to fall below this threshold under current fertility trajectories, with what implications for a passive-baseline (T5) versus managed-contraction (T2) approach?
content.why_it_matters: This determines whether passive demographic contraction (T5) will produce geographically uneven and potentially segregation-worsening outcomes that require active management (T2/T6), or whether demographic decline alone will resolve capacity pressures without policy intervention.
